name: ds-text-input
description: Single-line text entry control. Reflects value/placeholder/type/disabled/invalid; reports input and blur as events.

events.name: ds-blur
events.description: Fired when the control loses focus.

constraints.rationale: No primitive references, no raw values.

A standalone instance must still carry an accessible name (falls back to `name`, overridable by aria-label from a wrapping ds-form-field), clear AA contrast in both contexts, and never a positive tabindex.
Adaptive class: API drift is structural drift.

provenance.author: ai
provenance.justification: Design decision: `invalid` is a plain boolean prop the host sets, rather than the component computing validity itself (e.g. via the `type` enum's email/password shape or a `required` prop). Rejected alternative: giving ds-text-input its own required/pattern validation logic, mirroring native <input>. Rejected because this system already has a dedicated validation owner -- ds-form-field, whose whole job is deciding WHEN to validate against the ctx-driven validation-mode token (P4c) -- and a text input that validates itself would create two independent, potentially disagreeing validity computations for one field. `invalid` as a pure boolean keeps ds-text-input a controlled, dumb rendering surface: the field decides validity, the input just reflects it (border/focus-ring color change). This mirrors the same 'single source of truth' reasoning used for ds-label's required marker.
